headline.purpose: Page title (<h1> in PageHeader); the largest type on a normal screen. No hero/display tier above it.

body.purpose: All prose, sentence-case labels, descriptions (14px / 400). Capped at 65-75ch.

mono.purpose: Every literal value: key, size, id, address, count. Geist Mono with tabular numerals. The most common text in the console.

label.purpose: Table headers, meta, badges (12px / 500). Sentence case, never all-caps.

shadows.name: floating-layer
shadows.purpose: The only shadow in the system. Popovers, dropdown menus, dialogs, command palette, toasts: layers that leave the document flow and float above content. Flat surfaces never use it.

motion.name: ease-out-standard
motion.purpose: Default exponential ease-out for state transitions (hover, open/close, fills). No bounce, no elastic.

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: State transitions sit in the 150-250ms band; users are in flow, not watching choreography.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: prefers-reduced-motion collapses all animation/transition to instant; motion conveys state, so it degrades, never gates content.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single top-emphasis action on a screen. Neutral, never blue.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 0 16px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; background: oklch(0.205 0 0); color: oklch(1 0 0); font: 500 0.875rem/1 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.27 0 0); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.53 0.2 255); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary actions: a hairline border on the surface, neutral ink.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 0 16px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.922 0 0); border-radius: 6px; background: oklch(1 0 0); color: oklch(0.145 0 0); font: 500 0.875rem/1 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.97 0 0); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.53 0.2 255); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Destructive-Outline Button
components.description: A destructive-but-secondary action (e.g. Deactivate user). Red is carried by border + text, never text alone.
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 0 16px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.577 0.245 27.3 / 0.4); border-radius: 6px; background: oklch(1 0 0); color: oklch(0.577 0.245 27.3); font: 500 0.875rem/1 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-destructive-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.577 0.245 27.3 / 0.1); } .ds-btn-destructive-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.577 0.245 27.3 / 0.5); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard field: hairline border, 6px radius, focus ring (no glow).
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; height: 36px; padding: 0 12px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.922 0 0); border-radius: 6px; background: oklch(1 0 0); color: oklch(0.145 0 0); font: 400 0.875rem/1 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.51 0 0); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.53 0.2 255); outline-offset: 2px; border-color: oklch(0.53 0.2 255); } .ds-input[aria-invalid='true'] { border-color: oklch(0.577 0.245 27.3); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat surface separated by a 1px hairline; no shadow. Title (16px/600), description (muted), body.
components.html:
Storage quota
Cap how much this bucket can store, in bytes.
components.css: .ds-card { border: 1px solid oklch(0.922 0 0); border-radius: 8px; background: oklch(1 0 0); padding: 24px; } .ds-card-title { font: 600 1rem/1.4 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; color: oklch(0.145 0 0); } .ds-card-desc { margin-top: 4px; font: 400 0.875rem/1.5 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; color: oklch(0.51 0 0); }

components.name: Status Badge (active)
components.description: Semantic state pill: a tinted dot + label. Green only because 'active' is a meaningful positive state.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 2px 8px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.922 0 0); border-radius: 9999px; background: oklch(1 0 0); font: 500 0.75rem/1.4 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; color: oklch(0.145 0 0); } .ds-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: oklch(0.52 0.13 155); }

components.name: Mono Data Cell
components.description: How every literal value reads in a table: Geist Mono, tabular, near-black.
components.css: .ds-mono { font: 400 0.8125rem/1.4 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; font-feature-settings: 'tnum'; color: oklch(0.145 0 0); }

narrative.overview: Cairn is infrastructure: a self-hosted, S3-compatible object store that someone is trusting with production data. The console is the reason a person picks Cairn over MinIO, so its whole job is to make running your own storage feel calm and legible instead of intimidating. The look is Vercel/Geist restraint in service of that legibility, executed in React + shadcn/ui on the Geist Sans/Mono faces: pure-white (or near-black) surfaces, hairline borders, near-black ink, and one neutral primary button. Color is silent until it has something to say. This system is built on subtraction: depth comes from a single 1px border, not a stack of shadows; hierarchy from scale and weight, not boxes-within-boxes; the primary action is neutral black-on-white so the loudest thing on screen is a warning, not a Save button. The deliberate opposite of MinIO's cramped widget-soup.
keyCharacteristics: Pure-neutral surfaces (white / near-black) with hairline borders; depth is structural, never shadowy.
One neutral primary button. Blue is for links and focus only. Green/amber/red appear only as meaning.
Geist Sans for everything UI; Geist Mono with tabular numerals for every identifier, size, address, and number.
Fixed rem type scale (12 / 13px-mono / 14 / 16 / 20 / 24), never fluid clamp().
Spacious by default; dense only where data demands it, and calm even there.
Light and dark at contrast parity; motion conveys state, never decorates.

rules.name: The Meaningful-Color Rule
rules.body: Green, amber, and red appear only when they carry meaning (state, warning, harm). Forbidden as decoration or category coding. If a colored element doesn't change meaning when grayed out, it shouldn't be colored.

rules.name: The One Blue Rule
rules.body: Blue is reserved for links and the focus ring. Never a button fill, heading color, or background. The primary action is neutral; a tool full of irreversible operations must not teach the eye that blue means 'press me.'

rules.name: The Mono-for-Truth Rule
rules.body: Every value an operator reads as a literal (key, size, id, address, count) is Geist Mono with tabular numerals. Prose is Geist Sans. The font is the signal of 'this is exact data, not commentary.'

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Type is a fixed rem scale (12/13/14/16/20/24). Fluid clamp() headings are forbidden; no type element exceeds 24px.

rules.name: The 1px-Border Rule
rules.body: Structure is hairline borders, not shadows. If a flat surface reaches for a box-shadow to separate from the page, it's wrong: use the hairline border. A shadow on a non-floating element is a bug.

rules.name: The No-Nested-Card Rule
rules.body: Cards never nest. A bordered surface inside a bordered surface is two boxes competing; flatten to one, or separate with a divider and spacing.

dos: Do convey all structure with the 1px Hairline border (#e5e5e5 light, #2e2e2e dark). Reserve box-shadow for floating layers only (popover, dialog, menu, toast).
Do keep the primary button neutral. Make the loudest thing on a screen the destructive/warning state, not a routine action.
Do set every literal value (key, size, id, address, count) in Geist Mono with tabular numerals; keep prose in Geist Sans.
Do state plainly what an irreversible action will do before it happens, and confirm before harm.

Do use the shared vocabulary (Page/PageHeader, Card, DataTable/SkeletonRows, StatCard, StatusBadge, TextLink, FieldError, ConfirmDialog): one button shape, one form-control set, one icon style everywhere.
Do render ErrorAlert above retained content and skeletons only on first load; teach the interface in empty states, never 'nothing here.'
Do hold muted and placeholder text to real contrast (>=4.5:1), targeting AAA where it doesn't fight the task; honor prefers-reduced-motion.
donts: Don't use border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe. Full hairline borders or background tints instead.
Don't use gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, or a gradient hero. This is the generic AI-SaaS template Cairn rejects.
Don't drift toward consumer-cute / toy-like UI: no mascots, no playful illustrations, no bouncy or elastic motion, no emoji-as-UI.
Don't ship MinIO's utilitarian density: controls jammed together with no hierarchy or air.
Don't color anything that isn't carrying meaning; no blue except links and the focus ring; no full-saturation accents on inactive states.
Don't nest cards, reach for a modal as the first thought, or reinvent a standard affordance for flavor.
Don't use fluid clamp() type or text above 24px; don't set body or section labels in all-caps; don't add a tiny tracked uppercase eyebrow above sections.
